3 Oral Presentations from Our Faculty of Medicine to the International Continence Society Congress

19
						November

2025

We are proud to
announce that our fourth- and fifth-year medical students participated in the
International Continence Society Congress from September 17 to 20, 2025, with
three oral presentations entitled:

“The role of
artificial intelligence in diagnosing and treating interstitial cystitis /
bladder pain syndrome,” presented by Yara Allo (5th year)

“Energy
imbalance in microglia and astrocytes may be the leading cause of
hypersensitivity in central sensitisation in interstitial cystitis / bladder
pain syndrome,” presented by Tala Shabaneh (5th year)

''Chick
chorioallantoic membrane (CAM) model to identify angiogenic properties of
vitamin B12 and curcumin for interstitial cystitis / bladder pain syndrome,”
presented by Sümeyye Imal and Nour Boughrab (4th year).

Omar Gharbiya
(4th grade) also presented this study as poster at the 50th
Turkish Physiology Congress, held in Antalya from November 12-16, 2025.

These research
studies were conducted throughout 2024-2025 by Yara Allo (5th year), Elaf Al
Adrheai (5th year), Tala Shabaneh (5th year), Tala Shawabkeh (5th year), Ahmet
Lulu (4th year), Omar Gharbiya (4th year), Sümeyye Imal (4th year) and Nour
Boughrab (4th year) under the supervision of Prof. Dr. Güldal Inal Gültekin,
professor of physiology at the Faculty of Medicine.

Our students
delivered explicit scientific presentations at this prestigious conference and
obtained an invaluable experience, allowing them to engage with leading experts
and broaden their understanding of the field.
